rulings.Case Title: JANET ILG WINGERT AN INDIVIDUAL VS. ROBERT ILG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Approve Final Accounting Award Costs Of Partition And Apportion Partition Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 28, 2010, line 1, PLAINTIFF JANET WINGERT'S Motion To Approve Final Accounting Award Costs Of Partition And Apportion Partition Costs. GRANTED IN PART. REFEREE SHALL BE PAID FEES AND INTEREST. REMAINDER TO BE DISTRIBUTED IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PARTIES OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE PROPERTY. MOTION DENIED AS TO REQUESTS FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND COSTS. INSUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE FEES INCURRED WERE FOR THE COMMON BENEFIT. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PETER HEIN VS. CITY COLLEGE OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 28, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF PETER HEIN'S Motion To Compel Further Discovery Responses. GRANT IN PART AND DENY IN PART. DENY AS TO PRODUCTION NUMBER 3 BECAUSE DEFENDANT HAS RESPONDED. DENY AS TO PRODUCTION 11 AS OVERBROAD AND PLAINTIFF DOES NOT MEET THE HARDING LAWSON STANDARD. GRANT AS TO PRODUCTION 12 AND 13. DEFENDANT SHALL TURN OVER DOCUMENTS REGARDING THE DECISION TO HIRE DUBON, HOWEVER DEFENDANT SHALL NOT TURN OVER HER PERSONNEL FILE. GRANT AS TO INTERROGATORIES 12.6, 16.10, AND 204.3-204.7, INCLUSIVE. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED, DEFENDANT HAD SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION FOR ITS DECISION. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: LEV WEISBACH VS. CCS ARCHITECTURE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 28, 2010, line 10, Plaintiff's Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Re Issue Of Preliminary Injunction. MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. MOTION GRANTED AS TO SECTION 20(c) OF THE AGREEMENT (COVENANT NOT TO COMPETE). INSUFFICIENT EVIDENCE PRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF WAS COMPENSATED FOR GOODWILL. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN A LIKELIHOOD OF SUCCESS ON THE MERITS AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES TIPS IN HIS FAVOR. MOTION DENIED AS TO SECTION 20(D) OF THE AGREEMENT (EMPLOYEE NO-HIRE PROVISION). THE LAW ALLOWS PLAINTIFF TO HIRE EMPLOYEES IF THEY APPROACH HIM ON THEIR OWN. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN HE WILL SUFFER IRREPARABLE HARM IF HE IS NOT ABLE TO HIRE CCS EMPLOYEES, AND THE MOTION IS DENIED ON THAT BASIS. PLAINTIFF TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT OF $50,000. =(302/CWW)
